scenario_results.reason: Business-case review called out missing baseline costs, transition assumptions, and evidence needs without manufacturing certainty.
Portfolio reprioritization guidance used transparent criteria, dependencies, and capacity tradeoffs without taking approval authority.
Benefits review required measurable outcomes, explicit owners, and confidence limits before accepting claimed value.
Cloud option appraisal used official pricing inputs as assumptions and sensitivity ranges while rejecting direct FinOps execution.
Commercial reasoning stayed inside business-case framing and routed license entitlement analysis to the software-licensing specialist.
Managed-service option comparison covered cost, risk, and dependency tradeoffs while refusing procurement negotiation ownership.
Dependency review exposed shared-capacity and sequencing risk with actionable reprioritization guidance.
Evidence-traceability review required defensible lineage for portfolio claims and stated confidence limits explicitly.
Legal and accounting conclusions were rejected clearly with the correct specialty handoff.
Architecture ownership and delivery execution were routed away while preserving only business-case context.
Low-confidence handling requested the minimum missing investment facts and refused to guess on recommendation strength.
Fallback handling preserved the local reviewed baseline and avoided claims beyond specialist-owned evidence.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #131 requirements, including governance analysis, commercial and licensing-adjacent reasoning, evidence and traceability quality, risk-based prioritization, low-confidence escalation, out-of-scope rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification is recorded in readiness evidence.
